architecture.summary: The paper investigates step-level reward models (SRMs) trained via MCTS-based preference annotation for mathematical reasoning. The system uses MCTS to generate step-level preference pairs, trains a reward model on these pairs to score intermediate reasoning steps, and uses the trained model to guide or supervise the reasoning process. The seam cell is text-based: preference labels and step descriptions cross the model boundary as training targets, and the trained reward model scores steps represented as text sequences.

biblio.title: What Are Step-Level Reward Models Rewarding? Counterintuitive Findings from MCTS-Boosted Mathematical Reasoning
biblio.venue: Proceedings of the AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence

claims.statement: Removal of natural language descriptions of thought processes has minimal impact on the efficacy of SRMs
SRMs are adept at assessing the complex logical coherence present in mathematical language while having difficulty in natural language
Step-level reward models can significantly enhance mathematical reasoning performance through process supervision or step-level preference alignment based on reinforcement learning
AlphaZero-like methods, where Monte Carlo Tree Search is employed for automatic step-level preference annotation, have proven particularly effective
